data.title: Paradigm released its first prediction market poll, showing that approximately 36% of American voters are now prediction market users.
data.description: Paradigm VP Justin Slaughter posted on the X platform that Paradigm has released its first poll report of the year, which is also the organization's first survey on prediction markets. The results show that over one-third (approximately 36%) of American voters have used prediction markets, either by placing bets or browsing them for information. Furthermore, most Americans currently do not have a clear attitude towards prediction markets. Even among the 95% of Americans who have heard of prediction markets, nearly half do not have a clear opinion (including prediction market users). In terms of user demographics, prediction market users under 50 years old account for 66%, with those aged 18-34 making up 20% and those aged 35-49 making up 27%. Non-white voters show slightly higher usage rates than white voters, and men show significantly higher usage rates than women (46% vs. 31%).

data.title: UK to Seize Assets Linked to Singapore's Largest Money Laundering Case
data.description: The United Kingdom is preparing to confiscate assets in London valued at approximately 55 million yuan, connected to relatives involved in Singapore's largest money laundering case. According to NS3.AI, the case is associated with a group referred to as the 'Fujian Gang.' This move marks a significant step in international cooperation to address financial crimes and recover illicit funds. The assets in question are believed to be tied to individuals implicated in the extensive money laundering operation, which has drawn attention from authorities worldwide. The UK’s action underscores the global effort to combat money laundering and ensure accountability for those involved in such activities.

data.title: Polymarket's U.S. Platform Surpasses $750 Million in Trading Volume
data.description: Polymarket's U.S. platform has achieved a significant milestone, surpassing $750 million in nominal trading volume. According to NS3.AI, the platform has facilitated over 5 million transactions, highlighting its growing influence in the cryptocurrency market. This achievement underscores the increasing interest and participation in decentralized prediction markets, as users engage in various trading activities. The platform's success reflects the broader trend of digital asset adoption and the expanding role of blockchain technology in financial markets.

data.title: GLC New Energy Invests in Pharos for Renewable Energy Initiatives
data.description: Pharos has secured an investment from Hong Kong-listed GLC New Energy, valuing the company at approximately $1 billion. According to NS3.AI, the collaboration between Pharos and GLC New Energy will focus on renewable energy asset tokenization, decentralized energy trading, and carbon footprint verification. This partnership aims to advance the integration of blockchain technology in the renewable energy sector, enhancing transparency and efficiency in energy transactions.

data.title: Kalshi's weekly transaction volume surpassed 20 million for the first time, setting a new record for the platform.
data.description: Martins, a member of the KalshiData team, posted on the X platform that last week (the week of March 2 to March 8), @Kalshi's transaction count exceeded 20 million for the first time, setting a new record for the number of transactions in a single week since the platform's inception.

data.title: The assets of relatives of the "Fujian gang" involved in Singapore's largest money laundering case, worth approximately RMB 55 million, are slated to be seized by the UK.
data.description: Assets belonging to relatives of the "Fujian gang" in Singapore's biggest money laundering case are facing confiscation in the UK. The UK's National Crime Agency (NCA) reportedly intends to freeze and seize £6 million (approximately RMB 55 million) in assets held by a 24-year-old woman in London, including two apartments, a parking space, and six bank accounts. The reason given is suspicion that these assets are related to proceeds of crime and their source cannot be explained. The UK High Court rejected the woman's application to have the freeze order lifted on March 13. According to court disclosures, the 24-year-old woman's uncle is one of 10 people arrested, convicted, and sentenced in Singapore's S$3 billion money laundering case, while her father is among 17 other fugitives involved in the case and one of 15 people whose assets were later seized and handed over by Singaporean authorities. The court's verdict did not disclose the names of the woman, her father, or her uncle, but based on information previously released by Singaporean police, only the brothers Wang Shuiming and Wang Shuiting, originally from Anxi County, Fujian Province, China, match the details disclosed by the UK court. The woman is Wang Shuiting's daughter and Wang Shuiming's niece. (Caixin.com)

data.title: Bitcoin Transfer of $223 Million to Galaxy Digital Signals Potential Strategic Moves
data.description: A significant transfer of 3,146 BTC, valued at approximately $223 million, was recently made from an unidentified wallet to Galaxy Digital. According to NS3.AI, this movement could suggest large-scale trading activities, custodial operations, or strategic fund repositioning within the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: Ethereum Foundation Sets Operating Principles Focused on Privacy and Scalability
data.description: Vitalik Buterin announced that the Ethereum Foundation has established operating principles emphasizing censorship resistance, privacy, and technical self-sovereignty. According to NS3.AI, the foundation aims to prioritize CROPS and continue its efforts on scalability and account abstraction. These initiatives are part of Ethereum's ongoing commitment to enhancing its network capabilities and ensuring user autonomy.

data.title: Polymarket's 24-hour probability of a Fed rate cut before the June FOMC meeting has fallen 11%, currently standing at 28%.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that the probability of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates before the June FOMC meeting on Polymarket has fallen 11% in the past 24 hours, currently standing at 28%, down from a high of 39% previously. Trading volume for this event is nearly $1.8 million. Previous news indicated that the continued rise in core PCE is unfavorable to the Fed's dovish stance. Furthermore, the Fed is now expected to cut rates by 25 basis points each in September 2026 and March 2027, compared to previous forecasts of cuts in June and September of this year. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before they are priced in.

data.title: US Army Awards Anduril Industries $20 Billion Contract
data.description: The US Army has granted Anduril Industries a contract potentially worth up to $20 billion. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the agreement involves the acquisition of the defense startup's software, hardware, and services. This significant deal underscores the growing collaboration between the military and technology firms to enhance defense capabilities.

data.title: US military officials revealed that the war could continue for several more weeks, while Iran stated that Hargh Island is a red line.
data.description: US military officials have indicated that the war could continue for several more weeks. Furthermore, Iran has warned Gulf states that the attack on Hargh Island is a red line, potentially triggering a wave of attacks on the energy infrastructure of Iran's Arab neighbors. This is why Friday's US attack on Hargh Island, while potentially impacting Iran's oil export capacity, could also escalate the conflict further. Gulf states have recently attempted to lobby Washington against attacks on Hargh Island and energy facilities within Iran. In response, Washington has told Gulf diplomats that it will avoid attacking Iranian energy facilities and will pressure Israel to refrain from such actions. Hargh Island, approximately 8 square miles in size, houses an airport, oil terminals, and a port, serving as Iran's main export terminal. Its waters are deep enough to accommodate supertankers capable of transporting approximately 2 million barrels of oil (accounting for the majority of Iran's oil exports). Trump previously stated that the US "destroyed every military target on Hargh Island, the jewel in Iran's crown," but did not target the island's oil infrastructure. (Jinshi)

data.title: Crypto DAO completes open-source treasury contract and multi-signature governance deployment.
data.description: Crypto DAO announced that its core Treasury contract has been open-sourced and its multi-signature governance has been officially deployed. The Treasury contract is one of the core modules of the Crypto DAO protocol, responsible for managing reserve assets and authorizing the system to mint PRO tokens. Through open-source code, any user can verify the contract logic and operating rules in a blockchain explorer, achieving fully transparent on-chain auditing. Simultaneously, Crypto DAO has upgraded Treasury permissions to a multi-signature governance architecture. Under this mechanism, Treasury assets are no longer controlled by a single address; all critical operations require authorization from multiple signing addresses; and system permissions are distributed across community consensus.

data.title: Polymarket's US platform reported a notional trading volume exceeding $750 million and over 5 million transactions.
data.description: Crypto KOL dash posted on the X platform that, to date (Odaily Planet Daily note: starting from November last year), Polymarket's US platform has seen its nominal trading volume surpass $750 million, with over 5 million transactions. After reaching a peak of $2.6 million, its open interest is currently $1 million.

data.title: Planet Morning News
data.description: 1. Bank of America warns market trends resemble the eve of the 2007 crisis; 2. Trump: Economy will rebound immediately after the Iran war ends; 3. Sources: Trump administration seriously underestimates Iran's willingness to close the Strait of Hormuz; 4. Trump: US military has bombed military targets on Kharg Island, Iran's oil export hub; 5. CFTC Chairman: CFTC is developing standards and recruiting talent for cryptocurrencies, AI, and prediction markets; 6. Ethereum Foundation releases the "EF Mandate": emphasizing self-sovereignty and decentralization principles; 7. Crypto industry lobbying groups have spent approximately $271 million to influence the 2026 US election; 8. Wall Street Journal: Pentagon considers sending additional warships to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz; 9. British media: Some European countries consult with Iran to restore shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

data.title: The US SOL spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $7.5964 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the SOL spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $7.5964 million yesterday (March 13th, Eastern Time). Only the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF (BSOL) saw net inflows yesterday, with a single-day net inflow of $7.5964 million, bringing its total historical net inflow to $782 million. As of press time, the SOL spot ETF has a total net asset value of $855 million, a net asset value ratio of 1.68%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $969 million.

data.title: Iran Warns of Retaliation Against Attacks on Its Oil Infrastructure
data.description: Iran's Joint Armed Forces Command has issued a warning that any attacks on its oil and energy infrastructure will lead to retaliatory actions against the energy infrastructure owned by oil companies cooperating with the United States in the region. According to Jin10, this statement underscores the heightened tensions in the region, as Iran seeks to protect its vital energy assets. The warning comes amid ongoing geopolitical conflicts and highlights the potential risks to global energy markets. The situation remains tense, with implications for international relations and energy security.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Stocks Close Mixed as UPXI Surges Over 19%
data.description: U.S. stocks ended the trading session with mixed results, as crypto-related stock UPXI experienced a significant surge of over 19.3%. According to NS3.AI, the Dow Jones Industrial Average decreased by 0.26%, while the S&P 500 saw a decline of 0.61%. The Nasdaq also closed lower, reflecting a day of varied performance across major indices.

data.title: Meta Plans Significant Layoffs Amid AI Infrastructure Costs
data.description:
Meta is reportedly planning substantial layoffs that could affect 20% or more of its workforce. According to Odaily, this move is part of Meta's strategy to offset the high costs associated with its artificial intelligence infrastructure and to enhance the efficiency of AI-assisted employees.

Sources indicate that the exact date and scale of the layoffs have not been finalized. If the layoffs reach the projected 20%, it would mark the most significant reduction in staff since the company's restructuring at the end of 2022 and the beginning of 2023, which was termed the 'Year of Efficiency.'

data.title: U.S. President Trump Highlights Divergence in U.S.-Israel Goals on Iran
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has acknowledged that the United States and Israel may have slightly different objectives regarding Iran. According to Jin10, Trump emphasized the nuanced differences in the strategic goals of the two nations concerning Iran's activities in the region. This statement comes amid ongoing discussions and diplomatic efforts to address Iran's nuclear program and regional influence. The U.S. and Israel have historically collaborated closely on security matters, but Trump's remarks suggest a potential divergence in their approaches to handling Iran's geopolitical challenges.

data.title: Japan Hosts Indo-Pacific Energy Security Forum Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: Japan's Minister for Economic Revitalization, Ryo Akizawa, announced the commencement of the 'Indo-Pacific Energy Security Forum' in Tokyo over the next two days. According to Jin10, the event is co-hosted by the Japanese and U.S. governments, bringing together ministers and industry leaders from various countries. The forum aims to foster bilateral discussions and cooperation, which are particularly crucial given the current tensions in the Middle East.
